weeks.title: Static Feeding — Establishing the Pattern
objectives: WA delivers accurately to GA on the circle edge
GA holds position and presents a target
GS times their move to receive from GA
coaching_points: WA: deliver the ball flat and to the outside hand of GA — never lob over the defender
GA: square your body to the ball before you release to GS — no blind passes
GS: drive toward the post BEFORE the ball arrives — not after

activities.name: 3-Player Static Feed
instructions: Set up WA on the circle edge, GA at the top of the circle, GS inside
WA passes to GA. GA passes to GS. GS shoots.
Rotate positions every 10 shots
No defenders — pure pattern rehearsal

weeks.title: Feeding Under Token Pressure
objectives: WA delivers cleanly with a passive defender in place
GA pivots away from defender before feeding
Combination flows in under 2 seconds from WA to GS shot
coaching_points: GA: pivot on your landing foot — do not drag it
WA: hold the ball for a count of 1 to draw the defender before delivering
GS: if GA is crowded, cut across the circle to open a lane

activities.name: Passive Defender Feeding Drill
instructions: Add one passive (arms-only) defender on GA
WA must deliver to GA within 3 seconds
GA pivots and feeds GS — GS shoots
Score 1 point per goal — defender scores 1 for each 3-second violation

weeks.title: Full Combination — Live Defence
objectives: Execute the full WA–GA–GS combination against full-pressure defence
Develop a second option when the primary route is blocked
Maintain composure under contested shooting conditions
coaching_points: If GA is denied, WA hits the back option — Centre or second GA lead
GS: do not wait for the perfect pass — contest every ball that enters the circle
Coach: track goal accuracy — target above 60% from inside the circle

activities.name: 5v5 Circle Entry Game
instructions: 5 attackers vs 5 defenders in the attacking two-thirds
Goal: attackers must get the ball to GS for a shot within 5 passes
Defenders aim to force a turnover or 3-second violation
Play 4-minute periods — swap attack and defence

weeks.title: Pattern A — Wide Lead
objectives: WA leads wide into the centre third to receive from C
C delivers accurately to WA's outside hand
Rehearse timing so WA moves as the whistle blows
coaching_points: C: do not look at WA before delivering — draw the defender, then release
WA: your lead must start BEFORE the whistle — early movement wins the space
Everyone: know your starting position and secondary role if Pattern A is denied

activities.name: Walk-Through Pattern A
instructions: No defenders — all 7 players walk through the pattern at half speed
C passes to WA. WA advances. GA leads. WA feeds GA. GA feeds GS. GS shoots.
Repeat 10 times at increasing speed
Coach calls the whistle at different moments to test timing

weeks.title: Pattern B — Back Ball Option
objectives: Introduce back-ball option when Pattern A is denied
C reads the defence and selects between Pattern A and B
Players know their role in BOTH patterns
coaching_points: Pattern B: C turns and hits GD or WD who has broken into the centre third
WA: if your lead is covered, set a screen for the back option then re-lead
C: your decision must be made in 1 second — rehearse the read, not just the pass

activities.name: Two-Pattern Decision Drill
instructions: Coach stands at side and signals A or B JUST before the whistle
Team executes the called pattern
Progress to coach NOT signalling — C reads a single passive defender's position to decide
10 repetitions per pattern

weeks.title: Centre Pass Under Match Pressure
objectives: Execute both patterns reliably against full defending seven
Score from centre pass within 4 passes
Defenders intercept or force the ball out of the attacking third
coaching_points: Track centre pass conversion rate — target 60%+ in training
If C turns back twice in a row, reset and walk through the pattern again
Reward the team for a pattern goal — reinforce the system

activities.name: Centre Pass Conversion Game
instructions: Full 7v7 — each team takes 5 centre passes per set
Attacking team scores: 2 points for a goal from the centre pass pattern, 1 for any other goal
Defending team scores: 1 point for each turnover before the attacking third
Best of 3 sets

weeks.title: Reading the Passer — Anticipation Basics
objectives: GD/GK learn to watch the passer's eyes, not the ball
Identify the 3 telegraphing signs before a pass
Practice timing the move — too early loses the intercept
coaching_points: Watch the passer's shoulder turn — that tells you where the ball is going
Never turn your back on the ball — shadow the attacker from the inside
Move on the release, not on the catch — that is 0.5 seconds earlier

activities.name: Mirror & React Drill
instructions: Pairs: one attacker, one defender, 1.5m apart
Attacker has the ball and passes to a third player 5m away
Defender must read the pass and touch the ball line before it arrives
Count successes out of 10 — target 5+ by end of week

weeks.title: 1v1 Defence in the Defending Third
objectives: GD/GK apply correct 0.9m positioning consistently
Force attackers into the sideline or baseline
Contest every shot with arms raised — no slapping
coaching_points: 0.9m is measured from the foot on the ground, not from the body — know the distance
Goal: force the attacker to pass backward — do not lunge
On a shot: reach high with both arms — hands together above the ball line

activities.name: Defending Third 1v1
instructions: GA/GS vs GD/GK in the defending third only
Attacker gets 3 seconds to shoot or pass out
Defender must maintain 0.9m — any contact is a free pass
Count how many shots are contested vs uncontested

weeks.title: GK–GD Unit Combination
objectives: GK and GD communicate and cover for each other
One defender pressures the ball; the other covers the second attacker
No goals conceded from uncontested positions
coaching_points: Call 'mine' or 'yours' on every ball that enters the defensive third
If GD commits, GK shifts to cover — never both chase the same attacker
After a rebound, outlet quickly — do not hold the ball under pressure

activities.name: 2v2 Defensive Third Game
instructions: GA + GS vs GD + GK in the defending third
Attackers start with the ball and have 8 seconds to score
Defenders must communicate out loud before every move
Play 4-minute sets — rotate pairs every set

weeks.title: Landing & Pivot Mechanics
objectives: Every player can demonstrate a correct 2-foot landing
All players know which foot becomes the pivot foot
Pivot movements are smooth and controlled, not rushed
coaching_points: Land on 2 feet simultaneously — no step through
Decide your pivot foot immediately — hesitation causes dragging
The pivot foot may not leave the ground until the ball is released

activities.name: Footwork Gate Drill
instructions: Set 4 cones in a square, 2m apart
Player walks to each cone, receives a pass, lands, pivots 90 degrees, passes to next cone
Coach watches for any step — call it loudly
Progress to jog pace, then run pace
Target: zero foot violations in 3 consecutive circuits

weeks.title: Footwork Under Pressure
objectives: Players maintain correct footwork when fatigued
No footwork violations when receiving under a defender
3-second rule is met consistently while maintaining footwork
coaching_points: Fatigue causes footwork errors — work hardest at the end of training, not the start
If you are off-balance when receiving, regather before pivoting — do not rush
Your team loses a free pass every footwork infringement — emphasise the cost

activities.name: Fatigue Footwork Circuit
instructions: Players complete 30 seconds of high knees, then immediately receive and release under a passive defender
Coach tracks infringements — team does 5 push-ups per violation (bonds the group)
3 rounds of 6 players
Count violations at start vs end — improvement is the metric

weeks.title: Match Simulation — Footwork Focus
objectives: Transfer zero-penalty footwork into a full-game context
Referee all footwork violations in training games
Team target: fewer than 3 footwork penalties per quarter
coaching_points: Assign one coach/analyst to count only footwork violations per team per quarter
Display the running total to both teams during the game
Celebrate a clean quarter — team culture around no violations

activities.name: Footwork-Refereed Scrimmage
instructions: Full 7v7 scrimmage — all footwork violations are called immediately
No other rules are flagged — footwork only focus for this session
Track count per team per quarter on a whiteboard
Team with fewer violations in each quarter wins the quarter
